# @architecture/validate-package-metadata

Validates package architecture metadata against the governed JSON Schema.

## Package identity

```text
Name: @architecture/validate-package-metadata
Version: 0.1.0
Component: validate-package-metadata
Type: tool
System: architecture-governance
Domain: architecture
Bounded context: architecture-governance
Owner: architecture
```

## Lifecycle

```text
Stage: active
Role: tooling
Class: active.tooling
Catalog lifecycle: production
Visibility: internal
Support level: standard
Review cadence: quarterly
```

## Ownership

```text
Owner: architecture
Change control: owner-review
Promotion eligible: true
Semver policy: internal-traceable
```

## Responsibilities

- Load package architecture metadata schema
- Find package.json files under apps, packages, and architecture tooling
- Validate package metadata shape and selected cross-field rules
- Write JSON and Markdown validation reports

## Non-responsibilities

- Does not generate package README files
- Does not validate cross-package dependency graphs
- Does not perform lifecycle transition approval

## Public exports and usage

```text
Public exports only: true
Deep imports allowed: false
```

- Run from repository root with node tools/architecture/validate-package-metadata/src/index.mjs --check
- Use --root <path>, --format text|json, and --no-reports as required by ADR-0011 and ADR-0012
- Use --allow-missing-ajv only for bootstrap environments where dependencies have not been installed yet

## Boundaries

### Allowed consumers

```text
tooling
```

### Forbidden consumers

```text
external
```

## Operational notes

- Validation reports are written under reports/validation unless --no-reports is supplied
- Self-evidence is written under reports/tooling/validate-package-metadata unless --no-reports is supplied
- Ajv performs JSON Schema validation; semantic repository rules remain implemented in this tool
